Context: `open Causalean.Stat.MomentEnvelope` is in scope; `envelopeQuartic t q = t^4 - 2*t^3 + 2*q*t^2 - 2*q^2*t + q^2` (a def; `unfold envelopeQuartic` to expose it).

(1) `hasDerivAt_envelopeQuartic (t q : ℝ) : HasDerivAt (fun s => envelopeQuartic s q) (4*t^3 - 6*t^2 + 4*q*t - 2*q^2) t`.
Strategy: `unfold envelopeQuartic`, then build the derivative termwise and `convert ... using 1; ring`. E.g.
```
have e1 : HasDerivAt (fun s:ℝ => s^4) (4*t^3) t := by simpa using hasDerivAt_pow 4 t
have e2 : HasDerivAt (fun s:ℝ => (2:ℝ)*s^3) (2*(3*t^2)) t := (by simpa using hasDerivAt_pow 3 t).const_mul 2
have e3 : HasDerivAt (fun s:ℝ => (2*q)*s^2) (2*q*(2*t)) t := (by simpa using hasDerivAt_pow 2 t).const_mul (2*q)
have e4 : HasDerivAt (fun s:ℝ => (2*q^2)*s) (2*q^2) t := by simpa using (hasDerivAt_id t).const_mul (2*q^2)
```
then `((e1.sub e2).add e3).sub e4).add_const (q^2)` and `convert this using 1 <;> ring` (adjust the fun to match `fun s => s^4 - 2*s^3 + 2*q*s^2 - 2*q^2*s + q^2`). If the lambda shape differs, wrap with `show HasDerivAt (fun s => s^4 - 2*s^3 + 2*q*s^2 - 2*q^2*s + q^2) _ t` first.

(2) `envelopeQuartic_deriv_neg (v t : ℝ) (hv0 : 0<v) (hv1 : v<1) (ht1 : v^2<t) (ht2 : t<v) : 4*t^3 - 6*t^2 + 4*(v^2)*t - 2*(v^2)^2 < 0`.
This is a polynomial inequality; use `nlinarith` with product hints. Facts: `v^2<t`, `t<v`, `0<v`, `v<1`. Try:
`nlinarith [mul_pos (sub_pos.2 ht2) (sub_pos.2 ht1), mul_pos (sub_pos.2 ht2) hv0, mul_pos (sub_pos.2 ht1) hv0, mul_pos hv0 hv0, sq_nonneg (t-v), sq_nonneg (t-v^2), mul_pos (mul_pos hv0 hv0) hv0, ht1, ht2, hv0, hv1]`. If it fails, add more products of the slacks `(v-t)`, `(t-v^2)`, `(1-v)`, `v`, `t` (e.g. `mul_pos (sub_pos.2 hv1) (...)`, triple products), and/or raise `nlinarith (config := ...)` degree. It is a true inequality on `v^2<t<v<1`.

Use the lean-lsp MCP tools to check your proof (diagnostics/goal). When done, output a SHORT plain-text report: which decls you closed, which remain, and any blocker. No JSON required.
